ground_truth.title: Best closed headphones as of 07/2024
ground_truth.author: FutureBSD
ground_truth.main_content:
FutureBSD
Jul 5, 2024

Hey guys. I currently own Focal Bathys for my media consumption but I'm considering selling them and getting something with a wider stage and a better instrument separation. Main use case: Music (not limited to a specific genre) and occasional gaming. My profile for the new headphones:

- Closed headphones (wife-acceptance-factor at night)
- Wide stage
- Good instrument separation
- Neutral sound profile

As I'd be fine going for used headphones and keeping my eyes open for a while to get catch it at a good price I'd keep the price-range discussion open.

I don't own a dac / amp right now, which would be the next part of my package after choosing the headphones. I'd ideally prefer something portable (convenience factor), if it's able to provide the required power.

From what I've read DCA E3 and ZMF Caldera Closed seem to fit my profile and are pretty far at the end of what's possible right now? Anything else that's worth looking at and are there maybe additional questions that I should answer on my end to find the right headphones?

Thanks a lot in advance

PinkyPowers
Jul 6, 2024

Might also consider adding ZMF Atrium Closed to that list.

Monsterzero
Jul 6, 2024

The Caldera closed is killer. From the 3D-ness, to the detail retrieval, to the separation, to the stage, to the butter smooth mids...keeping it all very musical.

Youre going to need an amp to get the best out of them.

PinkyPowers said:
Might also consider adding ZMF Atrium Closed to that list.

I love the AC, but it's pretty far from neutrally tuned.

FutureBSD
Jul 6, 2024

I was afraid you'd all jump on the Caldera Closed. Is there anything else comparable to them from an other manufacturer?

PinkyPowers
Jul 6, 2024

I know a lot of people like the Audeze LCD-X Closed.

Personally, I haven't heard many good closed backs. And none of the ones mentioned so far. So I can't be of much help.

Currently, the best closed back I've heard and own is the ZMF Verite Closed. But it's not what I'd call neutral. It tilts dark and warm, but still possesses amazing detail and transient speed. The ZMF Atticus is a good, cheaper alternative, with more treble sparkle, but also more boomy bass. In other words, V-Shaped. Though it's not nearly as detailed. ZMF Eikon might work for you.

Another really good one is the Focal Radiance. They're less darkly tuned than the Verite, but the soundstage isn't huge. They're hard to find. I bought a used set for $700, and I'm glad I did.

The Sennheiser HD820 I have not heard. Some people love them, others hate them. Might suite your needs.

131359
Jul 6, 2024

Have you considered IEMs?

FutureBSD
Jul 6, 2024

131359 said:
Have you considered IEMs?

I do have a Moondrop Blessing 2 Dusk. But I never heard of an IEM with a wide soundstage. Besides from this I prefer something over-ear instead of in-ear.
